Klasse SyxDataStruct

java.lang.Object
de.btu.kt.syx.midi.SyxDataStruct
Alle implementierten Schnittstellen:
Serializable
Bekannte direkte Unterklassen:
SyxChecksum
public class SyxDataStruct extends Object implements Serializable
A structured system exclusive data buffer. Instances of this class typically represent
  • the body of a short system exclusive message, e.g., a parameter change message, or
  • a part of a large system exclusive message, e.g., a bulk dump.

Format Specifiers for System Exclusive Messages

System exclusive messages have a device-specific data format. For instance, the body1 of a Yamaha TG55 parameter change message has the format 

  43H 1#H 35H 0tH 0feecccc ppH[2] vvH[2]
with the parameters
•#:4 bits hardware device ID,
•t:4 bits structure type,
•f:1 bit element or filter,
•e:2 bits element number,
•c:4 bits channel number,
•p:2×7 bitsparameter number, and
•v:2×7 bitsparameter value.

The format of system exclusive message is defined by a message format specifier. A message format specifier is a white-space separated string of byte format specifiers:

  1. Hexadecimal: Two hex digits ∈ {0, ..., F} with upper-case letters, followed by an upper-case H. The first digit must not be greater than 7.
  2. Binary: Eight binary digits ∈ {0, 1}. The fist digit must be 0.

Any digit in the byte format specifiers can be replaced by '*' representing an arbitrary value. Further, any digit can be replaced by a lower-case letter ∈ {'a', ..., 'z', '#'} which defines a named parameter. Hence, at most 27 different parameters can be defined in one data struct (large SysEx messages can be partitioned into several data structs). Named parameters may span multiple bytes. The name '#' is reserved for hardware device numbers. A byte format specifier can be repeated n times by appending [n].

Example

The TG55 parameter change message above can be instantiated like this:

   String format = "43H 1#H 35H 0tH 0feecccc ppH[2] vvH[2]";
   SyxDataStruct prt = new SyxDataStruct(format);
   SyxMessage paramChangeMsg = new SyxMessage(prt);

Now we can write/read parameter values:

   prt.setMidiValue('p',0x10);
   prt.setMidiValue('v',0x01FF);
   
   int p = prt.getMidiValue('p'); 
   int v = prt.getMidiValue('v');

or write/read the entire data buffer:

   byte[] data;
   
   // ... Get the data 
   
   prt.setData(data); // Data must match pattern!
   data = prt.getData();

Footnotes

1 The body of a system exclusive message consists of all data bytes, excluding the leading status byte (0xF0 or 0xF7) and the tailing EOX flag (0xF7).

Autor:
Matthias Wolff

    Modifizierer und Typ
    Klasse
    Beschreibung
    static enum 
    SyxDataStruct.PTYPE
    Parameter types: SyxDataStruct.PTYPE.RAW: raw parameter without additional parameter information SyxDataStruct.PTYPE.RANGE: range of integer values SyxDataStruct.PTYPE.ENUM_INT: enumeration of integer values SyxDataStruct.PTYPE.ENUM_STR: enumeration of value names

  • Konstruktordetails

    • SyxDataStruct

      public SyxDataStruct(String format, String name) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Creates a structured system exclusive data buffer.
      Parameter:
      format - The format specifier
      name - The name of this data struct
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if format is null or invalid

    • SyxDataStruct

      public SyxDataStruct(String format) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Creates a structured system exclusive data buffer.
      Parameter:
      format - The format specifier
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if format is null or invalid

    • SyxDataStruct

      public SyxDataStruct(String format, String name, Object... params) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Creates a structured system exclusive data buffer.
      Parameter:
      format - The format specifier
      name - The name of this data struct
      params - A parameter name 'a', ..., 'z' or '#' (char) followed by a non-negative parameter value ( int); can be repeated
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if format is null or invalid, if the argument sequence is null or invalid, or if either argument is out of range
      IllegalArgumentException - if a parameter does mentioned in params does not exist in the format or if a value is too large to be stored in the data struct
      InvalidMidiDataException

    • SyxDataStruct

      public SyxDataStruct(String format, Object... params) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Creates a structured system exclusive data buffer.
      Parameter:
      format - The format specifier
      params - A parameter name 'a', ..., 'z' or '#' (char) followed by a non-negative parameter value ( int); can be repeated
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if format is null or invalid, if the argument sequence is null or invalid, or if either argument is out of range
      IllegalArgumentException - if a parameter does mentioned in params does not exist in the format or if a value is too large to be stored in the data struct
      InvalidMidiDataException

    • SyxDataStruct

      public SyxDataStruct(String format, String name, byte[] data, int start) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Creates a structured system exclusive data buffer.
      Parameter:
      format - The format specifier
      name - The name of this data struct
      data - A byte array to initialize the data struct from
      start - Zero-based index in data from which to start copying data bytes
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if format is null or invalid, if data is null, or if start is less than zero
      InvalidMidiDataException - if data does not match the data format, or if it contains too few bytes

    • SyxDataStruct

      public SyxDataStruct(String format, byte[] data, int start) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Creates a structured system exclusive data buffer.
      Parameter:
      format - The format specifier
      data - A byte array to initialize the data struct from
      start - Zero-based index in data from which to start copying data bytes
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if format is null or invalid, if data is null, or if start is less than zero
      InvalidMidiDataException - if data does not match the data format, or if it contains too few bytes

    • SyxDataStruct

      public SyxDataStruct(String format, String name, byte[] data) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Creates a structured system exclusive data buffer.
      Parameter:
      format - The format specifier
      name - The name of this data struct
      data - A byte array to initialize the data struct from
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if format is null or invalid, if data is null, or if start is less than zero
      InvalidMidiDataException - if data does not match the data format, or if it contains too few bytes

    • SyxDataStruct

      public SyxDataStruct(String format, byte[] data) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Creates a structured system exclusive data buffer.
      Parameter:
      format - The format specifier
      data - A byte array to initialize the data struct from
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if format is null or invalid, or if data is null
      InvalidMidiDataException - if data does not match the data format, or if it contains too few bytes

      public byte[] getData()
      Returns the data bytes of this struc. The returned array is copy of the internal data.

    • setData

      public void setData(byte[] data) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Sets the data bytes of this struct.
      Parameter:
      data - A byte array to initialize the data struct from
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if data is null
      InvalidMidiDataException - if data does not match the data format, or if it contains too few bytes

      public int setData(byte[] data, int start) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Sets the data bytes of this struct.
      Parameter:
      data - A byte array to initialize the data struct from
      start - Zero-based index in data from which to start copying data bytes
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if data is null, or if start is less than zero
      InvalidMidiDataException - if data does not match the data format, or if it contains too few bytes

      Returns the format specifier of a byte in this struct.
      Parameter:
      pos - The zero-base byte index
      Gibt zurück:
      The format specifier
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if pos is out of range

      Gibt zurück:
      A character array containing the parameter names, may be empty
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if pos is out of range

    • getParamNames

      public char[] getParamNames()

    • getParamType

      public SyxDataStruct.PTYPE getParamType(char pname) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Returns the type of a parameter.
      Parameter:
      pname - The parameter name, 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if pname is out of range
      InvalidMidiDataException - if there is no parameter pname in the format

    • getParamBitCount

      public int getParamBitCount(char pname) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Returns the number of data bits available for a parameter. If there is no such parameter in the struct, the method returns 0.
      Parameter:
      pname - The parameter name, 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the pname is out of range

      public void doAssertAllParamInfos()
      Verifies that all parameters of this struct have parameter infos.

      • getParamInfo(char)
      • addParamInfo(SyxParamInfo)

    • getParamInfo

      public SyxParamInfo getParamInfo(char pname) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Returns the parameter info of a parameter.
      Parameter:
      pname - The parameter name, 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      Gibt zurück:
      The parameter info, may be null if no information is associated with the parameter
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if pname is out of range
      InvalidMidiDataException - if there is no parameter pname in the format
      Siehe auch:
      • Ungültige Referenz

      • Ungültige Referenz 
        #setParamInfo(char, String, int, int, String)
      • Ungültige Referenz 
        #setParamInfo(char, String, int[], String[], String)

    • addParamInfo

      public void addParamInfo(SyxParamInfo pi) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Adds parameter information to a parameter. If information for parameter pi.getPname() is already present, it will be replaced.
      Parameter:
      pi - The parameter information
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if
      • pi is null
      • pi.getParent()!=this
      InvalidMidiDataException - if there is no parameter pi.getPname() in the format

    • getDefMidiValue

      public int getDefMidiValue(char pname) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Returns the default MIDI value of a parameter.
      Parameter:
      pname - The parameter name, 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if pname is out of range, or if there is no parameter pname in the format

    • getMidiValue

      public int getMidiValue(char pname) throws IllegalArgumentException
      Returns the MIDI value of a parameter.
      Parameter:
      pname - The parameter name, 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if pname is out of range, or if there is no parameter pname in the format
      InvalidMidiDataException

    • setMidiValue

      public void setMidiValue(char pname, int midiValue) throws InvalidMidiDataException
      Sets the MIDI value of a parameter.
      Parameter:
      pname - The parameter name, 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      midiValue - The MIDI value, a non-negative integer
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if either argument is out of range
      InvalidMidiDataException - if there is no parameter pname in the format, or if value is too large to be stored in the data struct

    • setMidiValues

      public void setMidiValues(Object... args) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Sets one or several parameters of this structured system exclusive data buffer.
      Parameter:
      args - A parameter name 'a', ..., 'z' or '#' (char) followed by a non-negative MIDI value ( int); can be repeated
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the argument sequence is null or invalid, or if either argument is out of range
      InvalidMidiDataException - if a parameter mentioned in params does not exist in the format, or if a value is too large to be stored in the data struct

    • getModelValue

      public int getModelValue(char pname) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Returns the model value of a parameter.
      Parameter:
      pname - The parameter name, 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if pname is out of range
      InvalidMidiDataException - if there is no parameter pname in the format, or if there is no SyxParamInfo for the parameter
      Siehe auch:
      • SyxParamInfo.midi2Model(int)

    • getModelValueAsString

      public String getModelValueAsString(char pname) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Returns a string representation of the model value of a parameter.
      Parameter:
      pname - The parameter name, 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if pname is out of range
      InvalidMidiDataException - if there is no parameter pname in the format, or if there is no SyxParamInfo for the parameter

    • setModelValue

      public void setModelValue(char pname, int modelValue) throws InvalidMidiDataException
      Sets the model value of a parameter.
      Parameter:
      pname - The parameter name, 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      modelValue - The model value
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if either argument is out of range
      InvalidMidiDataException - if there is no parameter pname in the format, or if there is no SyxParamInfo for the parameter, or if modelValue is not permissible
      Siehe auch:
      • SyxParamInfo.model2Midi(int)

    • setModelValue

      public void setModelValue(char pname, String valueName) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Sets the model value from a value name.
      Parameter:
      pname - The parameter name, 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      valueName - A string representation of the model value
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if either argument is out of range
      InvalidMidiDataException - if there is no parameter pname in the format, or if there is no SyxParamInfo for the parameter, or if valueName is not permissible

    • readString

      public String readString(String pnames) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Gets the values of multiple parameters as a string. This method is useful to read a sequence of characters from the MIDI system exclusive data.
      Parameter:
      pnames - The parameter names; either a range, e.g. "a-j", or a list, e.g. "abcdefghij"
      Gibt zurück:
      The midiValues
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if names is null, empty, or not a valid specifier
      InvalidMidiDataException - if any of the parameter names specified by names is not a parameter in the format

    • writeString

      public void writeString(String pnames, String values) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Sets the values of multiple parameters from a string. This method is useful to write a sequence of characters into the MIDI system exclusive data.
      Parameter:
      pnames - The parameter names; either a range, e.g. "a-j", or a list, e.g. "abcdefghij"
      midiValues - The midiValues
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if either argument is null or empty, if names is not a valid specifier, or if the length of midiValues does not match the number of parameters specified by names
      InvalidMidiDataException - if any of the parameter names specified by names is not a parameter in the format

    • isValidParamName

      public static boolean isValidParamName(char pname)
      Determines whether pname is a valid parameter name. Valid parameter names are 'a', ..., 'z' and '#'.
      Parameter:
      pname - The parameter name
      Gibt zurück:
      true if pname is a valid parameter name, false otherwise

    • validate

      public void validate(char pname) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Validates the current MIDI value of a parameter.
      Parameter:
      pname - The parameter name: 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if pname is out of range
      InvalidMidiDataException - if there is no parameter pname in the format, or if the current MIDI value is not permissible

    • validateAll

      public void validateAll() throws InvalidMidiDataException
      Validates the current MIDI value of all parameters
      Löst aus:
      InvalidMidiDataException - if validation fails

    • reset

      public void reset(char pname) throws InvalidMidiDataException
      Resets the value of a parameter to its default value.
      Parameter:
      pname - The parameter name, 'a', ..., 'z' or '#'
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if either argument is out of range
      InvalidMidiDataException - if there is no parameter pname in the format

    • reset

      public void reset()
      Resets all parameters to their default values.

    • match

      public boolean match(byte[] data, int len)
      Checks whether a data buffer matches this struct.
      Parameter:
      data - The data buffer
      len - The number of bytes to match, non-positive to match entire buffer
      Gibt zurück:
      true if the data matches this struct, false otherwise

    • match

      public boolean match(byte[] data)
      Checks whether a data buffer matches this struct.
      Parameter:
      data - The data buffer
      Gibt zurück:
      true if the data matches this struct, false otherwise

    • match

      public boolean match(SysexMessage sxMsg, int len)
      Checks whether a system exclusive message's body matches this struct.
      Parameter:
      sxMsg - The system exclusive message
      len - The number of bytes to match, non-positive to match entire message body
      Gibt zurück:
      true if the message matches this pattern, false otherwise

    • match

      public boolean match(SysexMessage sxMsg)
      Checks whether a system exclusive message's body matches this struct.
      Parameter:
      sxMsg - The system exclusive message
      Gibt zurück:
      true if the message matches this struct, false otherwise

    • createParamChangeMsg

      public SyxMessage[] createParamChangeMsg(String paramNames, char devNumParam, int devNum) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Creates system exclusive parameter change messages for given parameter names.
      Parameter:
      paramNames - The parameter names
      devNumParam - The parameter name of the device number in format, should be '#'
      devNum - The device number of the hardware instrument, (1, ...,
      Ungültige Referenz 
      AInstrument#getMaxDeviceNumber
      ()).
      Gibt zurück:
      An array of parameter change messages
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if devNum is out of range, if paramNames is null or invalid
      InvalidMidiDataException - if any of the parameter names specified by paramNames is not a parameter in the format of this data struct, if any parameter has no parameter info, or if the parameter info of any parameter does not provide a parameter change message format

    • createParamChangeMsg

      public SyxMessage[] createParamChangeMsg(String paramNames, int devNum) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Creates system exclusive parameter change messages for given parameter names. Equivalent to createParamChangeMsg(paramNames,'#',devNum).
      Parameter:
      paramNames - The parameter names
      devNum - The device number of the hardware instrument, (1, ...,
      Ungültige Referenz 
      AInstrument#getMaxDeviceNumber
      ()).
      Gibt zurück:
      An array of parameter change messages
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if devNum is out of range, if paramNames is null or invalid
      InvalidMidiDataException - if any of the parameter names specified by paramNames is not a parameter in the format of this data struct, if any parameter has no parameter info, or if the parameter info of any parameter does not provide a parameter change message format

    • addDataChangedListener

      public void addDataChangedListener(ISyxDataChangeListener listener)
      Adds a data changed listener to this struct.

      Listeners will be notified when the system exclusive data in this struct have been changed by one of the setData(byte[], int) or setData(byte[]) methods.

      The setParam⟨Xxx⟩ methods will not notify the listeners.

      Parameter:
      listener - The listener.
      Siehe auch:
      • removeDataChangedListener(ISyxDataChangeListener)

    • removeDataChangedListener

      public void removeDataChangedListener(ISyxDataChangeListener listener)
      Removes a data changed listener from this struct.
      Parameter:
      listener - The listener.
      Siehe auch:
      • addDataChangedListener(ISyxDataChangeListener)

    • equals

      public boolean equals(Object obj)
      Setzt außer Kraft:
      equals in Klasse Object

    • prettyPrint

      public String prettyPrint(String linePrefix)
      Pretty-prints this struct to a string.
      Parameter:
      linePrefix - A prefix string for printed lines (not applied to the first line)
      Gibt zurück:
      The printed string

    • prettyPrint

      public String prettyPrint()
      Pretty-prints this struct to a string; equivalent to prettyPrint("").
      Gibt zurück:
      The printed string

    • prettyPrintData

      public String prettyPrintData(String linePrefix, int globalPartIndex, int globalDataPos)
      Pretty-prints this struct as list of data bytes to a string.
      Parameter:
      linePrefix - A prefix string for printed lines (not applied to the first line)
      globalPartIndex - The zero-based printed part index
      globalDataPos - The zero-based printed index of the first data byte
      Gibt zurück:
      The printed string

    • prettyPrintData

      public String prettyPrintData(int globalPartIndex, int globalDataPos)
      Pretty-prints this struct as list of data bytes to a string; equivalent to prettyPrint("", globalPartIndex, globalDataPos).
      Parameter:
      globalPartIndex - The zero-based printed part index
      globalDataPos - The zero-based printed index of the first data byte
      Gibt zurück:
      The printed string

    • int_parseParamNames

      public final char[] int_parseParamNames(String names) throws IllegalArgumentException, InvalidMidiDataException
      Parses a specifier of multiple parameter names.
      Parameter:
      names - The parameter names specifier; either a range, e.g. "a-j", or a list, e.g. "abcdefghij"
      Gibt zurück:
      A character array of parameter names
      Löst aus:
      IllegalArgumentException - if names is null, empty, or not a valid specifier
      InvalidMidiDataException - if any of the parameter names specified by names is not a parameter in the format
